title: Dendrobium peruvianum
taxonomy: Dendrobium peruvianum is a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This family is one of the largest and most diverse plant families in the world, consisting of over 30,000 known species. Dendrobium peruvianum, as part of the Orchidaceae family, is further divided into various subfamilies and tribes. The genus Dendrobium itself is a large and widespread group of orchids that are commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Through its classification, Dendrobium peruvianum is part of the order Asparagales, which is a group of flowering plants that share similar characteristics.

intro: Dendrobium peruvianum is a species of orchid native to the Andean highlands, known for its delicate white flowers and medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in reducing stress, improving immune function, and supporting overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Dendrobium peruvianum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, Dendrobium peruvianum is used to prepare herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems, digestive issues, and menstrual irregularities.
